rationale: Both evaluations hold the score at 6/10 and surface the same core themes: strong full-stack/entrepreneurial profile, GISCollective as an AI-adjacent project, no advanced AI degree, and AI work limited to tooling rather than strategic platform leadership. The variant's wording differs (it credits 'AI-agent infrastructure work' and adds a director-level influence concern) but none of the differences key off Berlin vs. Bucharest — they are reworded restatements of the same skills-based critique and fall within ordinary run-to-run variation. No concern, score change, or reasoning ties to the location.
